observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the reciprocal accounts and effect the necessary adjustments, and to review the accounts on a regular basis in order to present a reliable and correct balances in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

Instruct the heads of the planning and concerned implementing office to:

a.1 Intelligently plan and evaluate the necessity, feasibility and timing of programs and projects implementation to be essential in the promotion of the general welfare in the community, and

a.2  Conduct prioritization of PPAs included in the AIP to determine the most appropriate schedule of implementation and to avoid possible delays during the execution of projects.

Subject to the availability of funds and the personal services limitations stipulated in Republic Act No. 7160, hire additional personnel or designate an existing qualified employee as an additional workforce to the Municipal Engineering Office or Municipal Planning and Development Office who could handle the project implementation in all aspect.

We recommended and management agreed to strictly comply with the rules and regulations of COA Circular 77-61 by setting controls and monitoring systems, to wit:

Prepare the required Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption complete with their attachments and submit to the Office of the Auditor for verification purposes and for determining the reasonableness of the fuel consumed for the period; and

Ensure the withdrawal slip for fuel, oil and lubricants are issued to government owned vehicles and their usage are authorized and appropriate, in order to avoid excessive and unnecessary expenditures.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to update the real property tax account registers/taxpayer’s index cards and submit the certified list of taxpayers to the Municipal Accountant in compliance with Section 20, Volume I of the NGAS Manual; and

Direct the Municipal Accountant to take up and adjust in the books the RPT/SET receivables based on the certified list, with a corresponding subsidiary ledger that will monitor the collections and delinquencies in order to present a more reliable balance in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Management:

Require the Human Resource Officer to evaluate the personnel structure and staffing requirements of each office and propose the hiring of a qualified personnel on the budgeted vacant positions; and

To regulate the hiring of Contractual and Job Order personnel by evaluating the necessity and urgency of needs and assess their individual assignments to avoid redundancy and duplication of work, and incurrence of excessive expenditures.

We strongly reiterated our recommendation that on the closure of the Municipal Dumpsite, provide for another area immediately and with urgency to where non-recyclable/non-recoverable materials and special wastes be disposed.

We recommended that the Municipality provide a separate appropriation for MCPC programs, activities and projects (PAPs) equivalent to one percent of its share from the IRA. Only PAPs enumerated in DILG Memorandum No. 2012-120 be charged out of the separate appropriation.

We recommended that management should exert effort in securing the records of the Accounts Receivable which remained outstanding for more than ten years and enforce its collection.  In the event that its collections are improbable, procedures prescribed pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-001 dated February 5, 1997 should be followed.

We recommended and the Management agreed to require the:
Municipal Engineer to develop a reporting mechanism directed to facilitate proper classification and recording of projects undertaken by the Municipality; and
Municipal Accountant to:
b.1	Prepare Journal Entry Voucher to reclassify the completed projects to its appropriate asset accounts and provide appropriate depreciation expense; and
b.2	Compute and prepare necessary adjusting entries to record the related accumulated depreciation from the period the building and other structures were completed and utilized.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Require the Inventory Committee to submit a separate Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Networks using the prescribed format within the prescribed period to facilitate verification and reconciliation of the asset;
Instruct the Acting-Municipal Treasurer to keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its component; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network, prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each component and properly disclose the details of the total road network system in the Notes to Financial Statements as prescribed by COA Circular No. 2015-008.

We recommended and the Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to review the details of the accounts and determine the cause of the variance.  Furthermore, prepare a Journal Entry Voucher to effect the necessary adjusting entries for the reconciliation of the two reciprocal accounts

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Notes to Financial Statements complete with appropriate disclosure in accordance with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2016-004 in order for the financial statements to be presented fairly.

We recommended and:

The Inventory Committee agreed to summarize and consolidate the result of the physical count of agency assets and prepare the required RPCPPE as a basis for adjustments and reconciliation of property records; and

The Local Chief Executive agreed to require the designated Property Officer and the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the reconciliation of the property records. Adjustments shall be made in the books or in the property records for variances noted or for proper disposition of properties.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to employ available means to locate the documents and to obtain information on the details of the transferred funds in previous years to determine the most appropriate action in order to comply with the set terms and conditions, otherwise, remit the unexpended amount to the Bureau of the Treasury.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to fully disclose in the Notes to FS the details of Prior Period Errors as required by Paragraph 54 of the IPSASs No. 3 in order to provide the users adequate information in understanding the financial status and capability of the agency through their FS.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant recognize in the books of account the receivables pertaining to market operations when they occur, in order to present fairly the balances of Operating Lease Receivable and Fees from Market Operations accounts in the financial statements based on the required standard
We recommended that the important gender issues or gaps identified for the year should be addressed or implemented in order for the gradual or complete elimination of the gender issues.

We recommended the following:

a.  The Municipal Treasurer exert more effort to improve the efficiency of collection of real property taxes. Avail of all collection remedies applicable in the current circumstance of the Municipality.

b.  The Municipal Treasurer issue warrants of levy and conduct public sale or auction of delinquent real properties. These are indispensable actions to avoid the criminal and administrative penalties provided in RA No. 7160. Coordinate with other local treasurers who had recently conducted public sale or auction in their own local government units to adopt their procedures and best practices.

c.  Management provide support to the Office of the Municipal Treasurer in the conduct of public sale or auction. Include in the Municipality’s appropriations the budgetary requirements of the activity and provide augmentation of personnel when needed.

We reiterated the following recommendations:

The Municipal Accountant prepare the following journal entry in the General Fund books to record the unrecognized PPE in CY 2016 to CY 2019:

Account Name		     Code         Debit         Credit
Specific PPE accounts      (Various)	xxx				    
Accumulated depreciation (Various)	                  xxx
Accumulated impairment losses(Various)	  xxx
Prior Period Adjustment    3-01-01-020	 	  xxx

To record unrecognized PPE in CY 2016 to CY 2019.
An item of PPE shall be measured using the cost model, i.e., at cost, less any accumulated depreciation and any accumulated impairment losses pursuant to Philippine Application Guidance (PAG) 2 of IPSAS 17.

For road networks, comply with the accounting guidelines provided in COA Circular No. 2015-008. 

Prospectively, the Municipal Accountant record projects constructed and/or procured out of the Trust Fund as PPE when they qualify to the definition and recognition requirements of assets and PPE as provided in IPSASs 1 and 17, and recognize the proper revenues accordingly.

We reiterated the following recommendations:

The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator set up and maintain a GAD database containing complete data requirements enumerated in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.

We recommended that management should exert effort in securing the records of the Accounts Receivable which remained outstanding for more than ten years and enforce its collection.  In the event that its collections are improbable, procedures prescribed pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-001 dated February 5, 1997 should be followed.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and other concerned LGU officials in charge of the disbursement and monitoring of the financial assistance should require the recipient agencies/organizations to submit a Fund Utilization Report together with supporting documents. Moreover, the recipient NGO should comply with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2007-001 to ascertain the attainment of objectives for which the funds were released as well as the propriety of expenditures.

We recommended the following:
The Local Chief Executive organize an Inventory Committee to take charge in the annual physical count of PPE and prepare the required RPCPPE for timely submission to the Office of the Auditor.
Require the Municipal Treasurer to maintain a complete and updated Property Cards to account for the receipt and disposition of PPEs, and to reconcile the same with the subsidiary ledgers maintained by the Municipal Accountant which should show the present value of the asset.
The Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the preparation of IIRUP and dispose the unserviceable properties to prevent further deterioration of the PPEs and as basis of the Accounting Unit to record dropping from the books of accounts.
The Municipal Treasurer to gather relevant information for proper establishment of accountability over the non-existing or unaccounted PPEs and require the Municipal Accountant to recognize the loss and set-up the corresponding receivable from concerned personnel.

We recommended that:
The Municipality request authority from the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) to authorize the Local Chief Executive to transfer the completed projects of prior years to the respective beneficiaries.
The Office of the Municipal Engineer provide necessary documents for the turn over and acceptance of completed projects already used by the recipient schools.
The Office of the Municipal Accountant prepare Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) for the dropping of affected School Building Account in the agency’s book and facilitate the transfer of records to the beneficiaries by taking up the assets received by them.

We recommended the following:
The Municipal Engineer furnish the Municipal Accountant of the Projects Status Report, Certificate of Project Completion, and Inspection and Acceptance Report of the completed projects as a basis for the transfer to appropriate asset account; and
Provide and periodically maintain the required ledger cards and subsidiary records of the assets already in use and recognize the accumulated depreciation thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer verify the status identified reconciliation items and take necessary measures to effect the adjustments.  Likewise, make representation with the bank for those reconciliation items that require their intervention and provide corresponding details of adjustments to facilitate verification.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer undertake the issuance and renewal of Property Acknowledgment Receipts every three years or whenever there is a change in the condition and custodianship of the asset in order to update the record of Property, Plant and Equipment and keep a copy on file.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive being the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council Chairman direct:
The LDRRMO to prepare and update annually the LDRRMFIP to include the activities to be funded from the unexpended QRF and DRRMF-MOOE of the previous years; and
The LDRRMO and the Municipal Accountant to ensure the timely submission of the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended the following:
The GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) through the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, set up and maintain a GAD database containing complete data requirements as enumerated in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01;
Adhere to the structure and composition of the GFPS which shall have an Executive Committee (Execom), a Technical Working Group (TWG) and/or a Secretariat. Membership for each of the groups under the GFPS to include individuals from different sectors and various offices/departments of the Municipality; and
Important gender issues or gaps identified for the year should be addressed or implemented in order for the gradual or complete elimination of the gender issues.

We reiterated to recommend the following:

The Local Chief Executive instruct the Inventory Committee to convene and take charge the annual physical count of PPE, and prepare the required RPCPPE for timely submission to the Office of the Auditor.

The Accounting Office and General Services Office/ Treasurer’s Office maintain and reconcile the required PPE ledger cards and property cards, and match the balances of PPE accounts per books with the RPCPPE.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor direct all concerned officials involved in the planning and implementation of 20 percent DF to evaluate the necessity, feasibility and timing of programs and projects implementation in the ensuing year to achieve the desirable socio-economic development as prescribed by DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

We recommended the following:

Prospectively, the Municipality strictly observe the guidelines on the receipts, utilization and reporting BGCM for the easy monitoring, and fully adhere with the rules to attain a unified, cohesive and orderly implementation of national policy to address an emergency.

The Management prioritize to update their official LGU website by posting the BGCM accumulated reports as required by Item 4.0 of LBC No. 125, and in order to promote transparency in government transactions.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to record the purchases of supplies and materials held for consumption as part of inventory;

Direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to maintain and update the Stock Cards and Supplies Ledger Cards, respectively, for each inventory item and corresponding issuances be covered by the RIS.  Likewise, the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI) should be prepared as a basis in recording the issued inventory; and

Direct the Inventory Committee to convene and conduct the periodic physical count of inventories, and prepare the Report of Physical Count of Inventories which shall be submitted to the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year in compliance with Government Accounting Manual for LGUs.

We recommended that the Management:

Ensure the transfer of unexpended balance of QRF and the LDRRMF – MOOE accumulated for years to the Special Trust Fund under the account Trust Liabilities – DRRMF in the Trust Fund books.

Determine the unutilized LDRRMF pertaining to calendar years 2012 – 2015 and revert to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund and made available for other social services after subsequent enactment of a local appropriation ordinance.

We recommended the following:

The Municipal Treasurer cancel the unclaimed stale checks and return the original Disbursement Vouchers to the Municipal Accountant in order to effect the necessary adjusting entries.  For the checks which became stale in the hand of payee, send written notice reminding them of the need to present their stale checks for cancellation before the replacement can be made.

The Municipal Accountant take up the necessary adjusting entries to record the stale checks and reflect the changes in the financial statements.

We recommended the following:

The Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to prioritize the processing of documents relating to the receipts and disbursements of government funds to facilitate the timely preparation and submission of financial reports and statements pursuant to the prescribed regulations.

Subject to the availability of funds and the personal services limitations stipulated in Republic Act No. 7160, the Municipality hire additional personnel or designate an existing qualified employee as an additional workforce to the Accounting Office and who could likewise act as Officer In-Charge whenever the Municipal Accountant goes on leave or on official travel.

We recommended the following:

The Municipal Treasurer prepare a list of obsolete and spoiled accountable forms and request inspection from the COA for proper disposal thereof.

We strongly reiterated the following recommendations:

The MENRO – Designate follow-up the approval of the 10-year Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan with the National Solid Waste Management Commission through the Environmental Management Bureau Regional Office. A new Plan be prepared and submitted for approval to update its 10-year coverage, if needed.

On the closure of the Municipal Dumpsite, provide for another area immediately and with urgency to where non-recyclable/non-recoverable materials and special wastes be disposed.

Coordinate with other local government units and the private sector in the establishment of a compliant sanitary landfill facility.

Consider the creation of a separate Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Office and the appointment of a full-time MENRO.

We recommended the following:

The Municipal Treasurer exert more effort to improve the efficiency of collection of real property taxes. Avail of all collection remedies applicable in the current circumstance of the Municipality.



The Municipal Treasurer issue warrants of levy and conduct public sale or auction of delinquent real properties. These are indispensable actions to avoid the criminal and administrative penalties provided in RA No. 7160. Coordinate with other local treasurers who had recently conducted public sale or auction in their own local government units to adopt their procedures and best practices.

Management provide support to the Office of the Municipal Treasurer in the conduct of public sale or auction. Include in the Municipality’s appropriations the budgetary requirements of the activity and provide augmentation of personnel when needed.

We recommended the following:

To facilitate the audit of the MSB’s compliance with the planning and budgeting provisions on the use of the SEF of DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1 s. 2017 in CY 2018, submit the required documents and reply to our questionnaire as soon as possible.

The MSB create its secretariat, with at least one personnel stationed within the Municipal Hall, so that concerns with regard to the utilization of the SEF may easily be communicated and/or coordinated with them.

We recommended that management submit the duly filled out Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation Form on the audit observations and recommendations embodied in the CY 2016 AAR to the Office of the Auditor. All items in the form be accomplished including the detailed action plan, person/department responsible, target implementation date, and status of implementation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer or with the concerned Department Head timely inform the Auditor through a letter that the appropriate signboard is already posted after award of an infrastructure project or before the start of a program/activity. Strict compliance to the prohibition on the display/affixture of items related to government officials on signboards and on maintaining and updating the project status portion of the signboards as provided in COA Circular No. 2013-004 as amended by COA Circulars No. 2015-006 and 2016-003 likewise be observed.

We recommended the following:

a.	The Municipality provide a separate appropriation for MCPC programs, activities and projects (PAPs) equivalent to one percent of its share from the IRA. Only PAPs enumerated in DILG Memorandum No. 2012-120 be charged out of the separate appropriation.

We recommended that management should strictly comply with the submission of contracts/purchase orders and all documents forming part thereof to the Auditor, within five (5) working days from execution to facilitate review processes, generate timely and relevant audit results and avoid enforcement of sanctions prescribed by law.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer be required to prepare the Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (CRAAF) and to submit the same together with the individual RAAF of all collectors in accordance with Section 50 and 51 of the NGAS Manual, Volume II. 
We also recommend that every time collectors will remit their daily collections, the Liquidating Officer/Municipal Treasurer shall require the presentation of the current and unused stubs to validate the last Official Receipt issued as appearing in the Report of Collection and Deposits and to sign   the last Official Receipt issued.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer should strictly require all collecting clerks to turn over their collections at the close of each business day. Ensure that all collections are completely remitted by checking the accountable forms daily. The Treasurer/Liquidating Officer should strictly comply with Section 32 of NGAS-Local to prevent any untoward incident to happen while so much cash is kept in the vault of the Treasurer’s Office. Compliance with the provisions of Sections 29 and 32 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for LGUs, Volume 1, should be strictly enforced, to protect government funds from risk of loss and/or misapplication.

We recommended the following:

Subject to civil service rules, to availability of funds and to the personal services limitation provided in RA No. 7160, consider hiring personnel possessing a certified public accountant (CPA) license so that an Officer-In-Charge (OIC) may be designated when the Municipal Accountant goes on leave or on official business, to provide continuity in the office’s transactions especially in the preparation and submission of accounts and reports. 

Likewise consider promoting qualified personnel in the Office of the Municipal Accountant to at least a second level position so that someone with professional eligibility can assist the Municipal Accountant in her duties meanwhile that there is no personnel with a CPA license hired.

We recommended the following:

To facilitate the audit of the MSB’s compliance with the planning and budgeting provisions on the use of the SEF of DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1 s. 2017 in CY 2018, submit the required documents and reply to our questionnaire as soon as possible.

The MSB create its secretariat, with at least one personnel stationed within the Municipal Hall, so that concerns with regard to the utilization of the SEF may easily be communicated and/or coordinated with them.

We recommended that management submit the duly filled out Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation Form on the audit observations and recommendations embodied in the CY 2016 AAR to the Office of the Auditor. All items in the form be accomplished including the detailed action plan, person/department responsible, target implementation date, and status of implementation.

We recommend that management should exert effort in securing the records of the Accounts Receivable which remained outstanding for more than ten years and enforce its collection.  In the event that its collections are improbable, procedures prescribed pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-001 dated February 5, 1997 should be followed.

The Local Chief Executive instruct the Inventory Committee to convene and take charge the annual physical count of PPE, and prepare the required RPCPPE for timely submission to the Office of the Auditor.
The Accounting Office and General Services Office/Treasurer’s Office maintain and reconcile the required PPE ledger cards and property cards, and match the balances of PPE accounts per books with the RPCPPE.

We recommended the following:

The Inventory Committee conduct physical count of the Municipality’s PPE annually and prepare the RPCPPE timely and comprehensively. Furnish the Report to the Municipal Accountant and the Auditor.



The Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant maintain the prescribed property cards and PPE ledger cards (PPELCs) respectively, and reconcile it with the RPCPPE.

As proof of reconciliation, the Municipal Accountant prepare at year-end a schedule/working paper showing that each and all of the PPE items per books were reconciled against the RPCPPE.

We reiterated the following recommendations:

The Municipal Accountant prepare the following journal entry in the General Fund books to record the unrecognized PPE in CY 2016 to CY 2019:

Account Name	             Code         Debit         Credit
Specific PPE accounts      (Various)	xxx			    			
Accumulated depreciation (Various)	                 xxx
Accumulated impairment losses(Various)	  xxx
Prior Period Adjustment    3-01-01-020	 	  xxx

To record unrecognized PPE in CY 2016 to CY 2019.

An item of PPE shall be measured using the cost model, i.e., at cost, less any accumulated depreciation and any accumulated impairment losses pursuant to Philippine Application Guidance (PAG) 2 of IPSAS 17.

For road networks, comply with the accounting guidelines provided in COA Circular No. 2015-008. 

Prospectively, the Municipal Accountant record projects constructed and/or procured out of the Trust Fund as PPE when they qualify to the definition and recognition requirements of assets and PPE as provided in IPSASs 1 and 17, and recognize the proper revenues accordingly.

We recommended the following:

Management institute necessary actions and remedies to compel the contractors to complete the unfinished projects immediately.  

The Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Accountant compute for and deduct liquidated damages from payments due the contractors of all construction projects not completed within their intended completion dates.

We recommended the following:

Accountable forms be kept in storage spaces with adequate physical controls and secured from humidity and other weather conditions, such as in locked metal cabinets.

The Municipal Treasurer or the person in-charge of accountable forms closely monitor that receipt and issuance of accountable forms are properly and completely recorded in the record book in the required form, and its release be duly acknowledged by the accountable officers with their printed names and signatures.

All accountable officers prepare the Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF) timely for consolidation and monitoring. 

Additional accountable forms not be issued to accountable officers who have not remitted their prior collections.

The Municipal Treasurer prepare a list of obsolete and spoiled accountable forms and request inspection from the COA for proper disposal thereof.

We recommended the following:

All grants and fund transfers by the Municipality to IAs and NGOs/POs be in consonance with COA Circular No. 1994-013 and COA Circular No.2007-001 respectively. Eligibility and entitlement of funds by IAs and NGOs/POs be supported with required documents.

We strongly reiterated the following recommendations:

The MENRO – Designate follow-up the approval of the 10-year Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan with the National Solid Waste Management Commission through the Environmental Management Bureau Regional Office. A new Plan be prepared and submitted for approval to update its 10-year coverage, if needed.

Create a Local Solid Waste Management Fund to which funds for the SWM program, which include the Municipality’s own appropriations, collections of SWM fees and fines, grants and donations, shares from public-private partnerships and incentives, be pooled. 

On the closure of the Municipal Dumpsite, provide for another area immediately and with urgency to where non-recyclable/non-recoverable materials and special wastes be disposed.

Coordinate with other local government units and the private sector in the establishment of a compliant sanitary landfill facility.


Improve the efficiency of collection, proper transfer and transport of non-recyclable/non-recoverable materials and special wastes.

Assist the barangays in enforcing waste segregation at the household/establishment level and in the establishment and operation of materials recovery facilities (MRFs).

Consider the creation of a separate Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Office and the appointment of a full-time MENRO.

We reiterated the following recommendations:

Re-organize the Municipality’s GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to be composed of an Executive Committee (Execom), a Technical Working Group (TWG) and/or a Secretariat. Membership for each of the groups under the GFPS to include individuals from different sectors and various offices/departments of the Municipality.

The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator set up and maintain a GAD database containing complete data requirements enumerated in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01. 

The GAD Accomplishment Report (AR) be prepared in the required form and timely submitted for review and consolidation in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, as amended by PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01.

We recommended that management ensure that all payments of allowances, incentives and other fringe benefits to government officials and employees be supported with legal basis and conform strictly with the laws, rules and regulations granting or authorizing such payments.

We recommended the following:

The Municipal Treasurer exert more effort to improve the efficiency of collection of real property taxes. Avail of all collection remedies applicable in the current circumstance of the Municipality.


The Municipal Treasurer issue warrants of levy and conduct public sale or auction of delinquent real properties. These are indispensable actions to avoid the criminal and administrative penalties provided in RA No. 7160. Coordinate with other local treasurers who had recently conducted public sale or auction in their own local government units to adopt their procedures and best practices.

Management provide support to the Office of the Municipal Treasurer in the conduct of public sale or auction. Include in the Municipality’s appropriations the budgetary requirements of the activity and provide augmentation of personnel when needed.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer or with the concerned Department Head timely inform the Auditor through a letter that the appropriate signboard is already posted after award of an infrastructure project or before the start of a program/activity. Strict compliance to the prohibition on the display/affixture of items related to government officials on signboards and on maintaining and updating the project status portion of the signboards as provided in COA Circular No. 2013-004 as amended by COA Circulars No. 2015-006 and 2016-003 likewise be observed.

We recommended the following:

a.	The Municipality provide a separate appropriation for MCPC programs, activities and projects (PAPs) equivalent to one percent of its share from the IRA. Only PAPs enumerated in DILG Memorandum No. 2012-120 be charged out of the separate appropriation.

We recommended that Management create a separate Municipal Tourism Office with its own appropriations. Collections derived from tourism activities be utilized for tourism investments, projects and activities to sustain the viability of the TEA Park as a tourism enterprise.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the MPDC to conduct regular monitoring and evaluation of the 20% Development Fund projects in order to achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes for the benefit of the intended beneficiaries. Also, mobilize the concerned offices in the early planning and implementation of development projects to avoid incurring possible additional cost resulting in delayed implementation.

We recommend that management exert effort to enforce the immediate settlement of audit disallowances by sending demand letters to the persons liable. For those personnel who are still in service, the Local Chief Executive should demand the immediate settlement of disallowance by directing the Municipal Treasurer to withhold payment of the salary and other emoluments to the satisfaction of the amounts disallowed.

We recommend that the Accounting Office review the composition of the Construction in Progress account and adjust those that are already complete.  Henceforth, observe the proper accounting of assets constructed by the municipality as provided in the above regulation.

We recommend that management should strictly comply with the submission of contracts/purchase orders and all documents forming part thereof to the Auditor, within five (5) working days from execution to facilitate review processes, generate timely and relevant audit results and avoid enforcement of sanctions prescribed by law.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer be required to prepare the Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (CRAAF) and to submit the same together with the individual RAAF of all collectors in accordance with Section 50 and 51 of the NGAS Manual, Volume II. 
We also recommend that every time collectors will remit their daily collections, the Liquidating Officer/Municipal Treasurer shall require the presentation of the current and unused stubs to validate the last Official Receipt issued as appearing in the Report of Collection and Deposits and to sign   the last Official Receipt issued.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer should strictly require all collecting clerks to turn over their collections at the close of each business day. Ensure that all collections are completely remitted by checking the accountable forms daily. The Treasurer/Liquidating Officer should strictly comply with Section 32 of NGAS-Local to prevent any untoward incident to happen while so much cash is kept in the vault of the Treasurer’s Office. Compliance with the provisions of Sections 29 and 32 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for LGUs, Volume 1, should be strictly enforced, to protect government funds from risk of loss and/or misapplication.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant and other concerned LGU officials in charge of the disbursement and monitoring of the financial assistance should require the recipient agencies/ organizations to submit a Fund Utilization Report together with supporting documents. Moreover, the recipient NGO should comply with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2007-001 to ascertain the attainment of objectives for which the funds were released as well as the propriety of expenditures.

We recommended the following:

To facilitate the audit of the MSB’s compliance with the planning and budgeting provisions on the use of the SEF of DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1 s. 2017 in CY 2018, submit the required documents and reply to our questionnaire as soon as possible.

The MSB create its secretariat, with at least one personnel stationed within the Municipal Hall, so that concerns with regard to the utilization of the SEF may easily be communicated and/or coordinated with them.

We recommended that management submit the duly filled out Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation Form on the audit observations and recommendations embodied in the CY 2016 AAR to the Office of the Auditor. All items in the form be accomplished including the detailed action plan, person/department responsible, target implementation date, and status of implementation.

We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator set up and maintain a GAD database containing complete data requirements enumerated in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01. The GFPS TWG use sex-disaggregated data and other GAD-related information in developing a gender-responsive GAD Plan and Budget.

We recommended the following:

All salary claims of appointive officials and employees be supported with DTRs and all other documents showing proof of service.

Strict policy on the timely submission of DTRs be issued and implemented. Department heads exercise due diligence in monitoring timely submission of DTRs by their subordinates.

To facilitate timely and accurate recording and monitoring of attendance, consider the use of bundy clocks or biometrics technology.
Withhold salaries of officials or employees who failed to submit DTRs and/or other documents showing proof of service. Administrative action likewise be instituted against non-complying officials and employees following civil service laws and rules.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive issue a policy and the Sangguniang Bayan pass a resolution regulating mobile communication usage and expenditures of elective officials and department heads. The allocated limit of mobile communication expenses per official/head should be reasonable and not excessive.

Institutionalize the creation of the Municipality’s GFPS, composed of an Execom, a TWG and/or a Secretariat. Each of the groups under the GFPS be composed of individuals from different sectors and various offices/departments of the Municipality.

The GFPS to actively perform its functions, roles and responsibilities in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 as amended by PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01 especially in the timely preparation and submission of the GPB.

We recommended the following:

Properly plan for and maximize access to all allowed sources of financing for solid waste management projects and activities as provided in the IRR of RA No. 9003.



Follow-up the status of the full approval of the 10-year Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan by the National Solid Waste Management Commission. Once fully approved, avail the National Solid Waste Management Fund to implement additional solid waste management projects and activities not funded by the Municipality’s regular appropriations and/or other sources.

Create a Local Solid Waste Management Fund. Funds that will constitute it be sourced from the Municipality’s appropriations, collections of solid waste management fees and fines, grants and donations, shares from public-private partnerships, and incentives. The Fund be used solely for solid waste management projects and activities.

Prioritize funding for the maintenance and operation of the Municipality’s residual containment area (RCA).

We recommended the following:

Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses account (Account Number 5-02-99-990) be used only to record expenses not falling under any of the specific maintenance and other operating expense accounts.

Grants to Non-Government Organizations/People’s Organizations (NGOs/POs) for the implementation of specific projects be recorded as Due from NGOs/POs first and recorded only as expense upon liquidation.

In the preparation of the Municipality’s budgets, avoid including programs, activities and projects in lump-sum amounts. Instead, each program, activity or project be provided with specific accounts as provided in the Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units.

We recommended the following:

Responsible officials, as indicated in Table I above, immediately submit accounts and reports to the Office of the Auditor to facilitate timely conduct of audit. Observe strictly the provisions of PD No. 1445, pertinent COA circulars and the MNGAS for LGUs on the submission of accounts and reports.

The procedure records the actual arrival and departure of employees in the office, thus avoiding overpayment in salaries and wages.  For future transactions, avoid purchasing supplies and equipments when its use is not immediate, to avoid wastage of government resources.

We recommend that management should exert effort in securing the records of the Accounts Receivable which remained outstanding for more than ten years and enforce its collection.  In the event that its collections are improbable, procedures prescribed pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-001 dated February 5, 1997 should be followed.

The Inventory Committee prepare the RPCPPE timely and comprehensively. Furnish the Report to the Municipal Accountant and the Auditor.
The Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant maintain the prescribed property cards and PPE ledger cards (PPELCs) respectively, and reconcile it with the RPCPPE.
As proof of reconciliation, the Municipal Accountant prepare at year-end a schedule/working paper showing that each and all of the PPE items per books were reconciled against the RPCPPE.

We recommended the following:
The Municipal Accountant prepare the following journal entry in the General Fund books to record the unrecognized PPE in CY 2016 to CY 2018:
Account Name			Code			Debit		Credit
Specific PPE accounts			(Various)			xxx
Accumulated depreciation	(Various)					xxx
Accumulated impairment losses	(Various)				xxx
Prior Period Adjustment		3-01-01-020			xxx
To record unrecognized PPE in CY 2016 to CY 2018.
An item of PPE shall be measured using the cost model, i.e., at cost, less any accumulated depreciation and any accumulated impairment losses pursuant to Philippine Application Guidance (PAG) 2 of PPSAS 17.
For road networks, comply with the accounting guidelines provided in COA Circular No. 2015-008.
Prospectively, the Municipal Accountant record projects constructed and/or procured out of the Trust Fund as PPE when they qualify to the definition and recognition requirements of assets and PPE as provided in PPSASs 1 and 17, and recognize the proper revenues accordingly.

We recommended the following:
The Municipal Accountant record infrastructure assets as PPE in accordance with PPSAS 17. Provision for depreciation and impairment for these infrastructure assets shall follow the same procedures as other PPE items.
The Municipal Accountant prepare the following journal entry to correct the recording of infrastructure assets misclassified as expenses in CY 2018:
Account Name				 Code		Debit		Credit
Specific infrastructure asset account	      (Various)     10,028,690.26
Prior Period Adjustment		    3-01-01-020			 10,028,690.26
To correct recording of infrastructure assets recorded as Repairs and Maintenance - Infrastructure Assets instead of specific infrastructure asset accounts under PPE in CY 2018.
Furthermore, the Municipal Accountant review infrastructure assets recorded as expenses in years prior to CY 2018 and prepare correcting journal entries accordingly.
Specifically on road networks, observe the accounting and reporting guidelines on the local roads asset management system as provided in COA Circular No. 2015-008.
The Inventory Committee include in its conduct of physical count of PPE and in its report thereof infrastructure assets of the Municipality. Infrastructure assets listed in the report be reconciled to accounting records and be made as basis in recording to the books of accounts.

We recommended the following:
Management provide justification why payment of materials for the construction of a fountain system in the municipal plaza was charged against the 20% Development Fund.
Observe guidelines provided in DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund.

We recommended the following:
Utilize the LDRRMF solely for DRRM-related projects and activities provided in NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73.
Appropriate a separate budget for the operations of the Municipal DRRM Office from where administrative expenditures for personnel services, maintenance and other operating expenses and capital outlay be charged.
Budgetary requirements of the Emergency Response Unit (ERU) and the Incident Command System (ICS) of the Municipality be sourced from the different offices of the Municipality found to have crucial roles in its operation and not solely from the LDRRMF. The budgetary share of the ERU and ICS from the LDRRMF should be strictly DRRM-related, e.g., disaster operations center and early warning system, as defined in regulations.

We recommended the following:
All salary claims of appointive officials and employees be supported with DTRs and all other documents showing proof of service.
In the case of salary claims of elective officials, attach approved applications for leave and/or travel authorities and certificates of appearance. Specifically, for members of the Sangguniang Bayan, include minutes of the sessions and/or entries of attendance in the logbook.
Strict policy on the timely submission of DTRs be issued and implemented. Department heads exercise due diligence in monitoring timely submission of DTRs by their subordinates.
To facilitate timely and accurate recording and monitoring of attendance, consider the use of bundy clocks or biometrics technology.
Withhold salaries of officials or employees who failed to submit DTRs and/or other documents showing proof of service. Administrative action likewise be instituted against non-complying officials and employees following civil service laws and rules.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive issue a policy and the Sangguniang Bayan pass a resolution regulating mobile communication usage and expenditures of elective officials and department heads. The allocated limit of mobile communication expenses per official/head should be reasonable and not excessive.

We recommended the following:
The Municipal Treasurer exert more effort to improve the efficiency of collection of real property taxes. Avail of all collection remedies applicable in the current circumstance of the Municipality.
The Municipal Treasurer issue warrants of levy and conduct public sale or auction of delinquent real properties. These are indispensable actions to avoid the criminal and administrative penalties provided in RA No. 7160. Coordinate with other local treasurers who had recently conducted public sale or auction in their own local government units to adopt their procedures and best practices.
Management provide support to the Office of the Municipal Treasurer in the conduct of public sale or auction. Include in the Municipality’s appropriations the budgetary requirements of the activity and provide augmentation of personnel when needed.

We recommended the following:
Institutionalize the creation of the Municipality’s GFPS, composed of an Execom, a TWG and/or a Secretariat. Each of the groups under the GFPS be composed of individuals from different sectors and various offices/departments of the Municipality.
The GFPS to actively perform its functions, roles and responsibilities in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 as amended by PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01 especially in the timely preparation and submission of the GPB.

We recommended the following:
Properly plan for and maximize access to all allowed sources of financing for solid waste management projects and activities as provided in the IRR of RA No. 9003.
Follow-up the status of the full approval of the 10-year Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan by the National Solid Waste Management Commission. Once fully approved, avail the National Solid Waste Management Fund to implement additional solid waste management projects and activities not funded by the Municipality’s regular appropriations and/or other sources.
Create a Local Solid Waste Management Fund. Funds that will constitute it be sourced from the Municipality’s appropriations, collections of solid waste management fees and fines, grants and donations, shares from public-private partnerships, and incentives. The Fund be used solely for solid waste management projects and activities.
Prioritize funding for the maintenance and operation of the Municipality’s residual containment area (RCA).

We recommended the following:
Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses account (Account Number 5-02-99-990) be used only to record expenses not falling under any of the specific maintenance and other operating expense accounts.
Grants to Non-Government Organizations/People’s Organizations (NGOs/POs) for the implementation of specific projects be recorded as Due from NGOs/POs first and recorded only as expense upon liquidation.
In the preparation of the Municipality’s budgets, avoid including programs, activities and projects in lump-sum amounts. Instead, each program, activity or project be provided with specific accounts as provided in the Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units.

We recommended the following:
Responsible officials, as indicated in Table I above, immediately submit accounts and reports to the Office of the Auditor to facilitate timely conduct of audit. Observe strictly the provisions of PD No. 1445, pertinent COA circulars and the MNGAS for LGUs on the submission of accounts and reports.
Subject to civil service rules, to availability of funds and to the personal services limitation provided in RA No. 7160, consider hiring personnel possessing a certified public accountant (CPA) license so that an Officer-In-Charge (OIC) may be designated when the Municipal Accountant goes on leave or on official business, to provide continuity in the office’s transactions especially in the preparation and submission of accounts and reports.
Likewise consider promoting qualified personnel in the Office of the Municipal Accountant to at least a second level position so that someone with professional eligibility can assist the Municipal Accountant in her duties meanwhile that there is no personnel with a CPA license hired.

We recommended the following:
To facilitate the audit of the MSB’s compliance with the planning and budgeting provisions on the use of the SEF of DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1 s. 2017 in CY 2018, submit the required documents and reply to our questionnaire as soon as possible.
The MSB create its secretariat, with at least one personnel stationed within the Municipal Hall, so that concerns with regard to the utilization of the SEF may easily be communicated and/or coordinated with them.

We recommended that Management direct persons liable for disallowances, and persons responsible in case of audit suspensions, to immediately settle their accounts to be relieved of their liabilities or responsibilities respectively. Settlement of disallowances through installment payments should be in accordance with COA Resolution No. 2017-021.

We recommended the following:

The Inventory Committee conduct physical count of the Municipality’s PPE annually.

Prepare promptly the RPCPPE and submit to the Office of the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year.




The Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant maintain the prescribed stock cards/property cards and (SLC)/(PPELC) respectively, and reconcile it with the RPCPPE to establish the correct amount of the PPE in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management prioritize and hasten processing of transfer of land titles in the name of the Municipality. To secure ownership on succeeding purchase of lots, payment be made only after the title has been transferred in the name of the Municipality.

We recommended the following:

a.	Maximize utilization of the unexpended prior years’ LDRRMF for disaster-related PAPs.




b.	Include PAPs to be funded out of the unexpended prior years’ LDRRMF in the LDRRMF Investment Plan of the Municipality. Synchronize and consolidate planning for and utilization of the unexpended prior years’ LDRRMF with that of the current year LDRRMF. The form of the Investment Plan be in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-002. 

Re-program and/or update PAPs to be funded out of the unexpended prior years’ LDRRMF every year to reflect the current DRRM needs of the Municipality.

We recommended that responsible officials immediately submit accounts and reports to the Office of the Auditor to facilitate timely conduct of audit. Provisions of PD No. 1445, pertinent COA circulars and the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs on the submission of accounts and reports be strictly observed.

We recommended the following:


a. 	The MENRO-Designate, in coordination with other offices of the Municipality, strictly implement and enforce the provisions of RA No. 9003 and its IRR, and the Municipality’s local ordinance in the segregation, collection and disposal of wastes.


b. 	Provide assistance to and monitor the barangays in the establishment and operation of materials recovery facilities (MRFs). 

c. 	The Municipality’s garbage trucks collect only non-recyclable/non-recoverable materials in the MRFs of the barangays for transport to the residual containment area (RCA). Require the public market, schools, business other establishments to likewise establish and maintain their MRFs.

We recommended that Management expedite the processing of documentary requirements needed for the implementation of the construction of the potable water supply system and immediately submit the required notarized Certification and implementation schedule to the DILG Regional Office. Strict compliance to DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-3 is enjoined.

We recommended the following:

a.	The Municipality provide a separate appropriation for MCPC programs, activities and projects (PAPs) equivalent to one percent of its share from the IRA. Only PAPs enumerated in DILG Memorandum No. 2012-120 be charged out of the separate appropriation.


b.	The MCPC be more active in planning for, implementing and monitoring PAPs on children. Create a secretariat who will perform administrative work and prepare reports for the Council.

c.	PAPs on children be complete and inclusive, and to contain programs to assist children in need of special protection (CNSP) and intervention and diversion programs for children in conflict with the law (CICL) among others.

We recommended that Management create a separate Municipal Tourism Office with its own appropriations. Collections derived from tourism activities be utilized for tourism investments, projects and activities to sustain the viability of the TEA Park as a tourism enterprise.

We recommended the following:

a. 	Prepare and submit for review and endorsement the GPB timely and in accordance with the schedule set in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Circular No. 2016-01. 


b. 	Attach the results of the HGDG assessment and other relevant documents to support attribution of regular programs and projects of the Municipality to the GPB.


c. 	Integrate the gender issues and concerns identified in the duly reviewed and endorsed GPB in the formulation of the Municipality’s annual budget and in the preparation/updating/enhancement of other plans.
We recommended that Management reconcile the balances by securing billing statements and confirm with the BIR if the Municipality has outstanding unremitted amounts.

We recommended that the management cancel checks long outstanding in the General Fund (GF) and Special Education Fund (SEF), revert the amounts to the unappropriated surplus of the fund and adjust its books for fair presentation of Cash in Bank in the Financial Statements pursuant to the provision of Section 59 of the New Government Accounting Manual System (NGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs) Volume 1 and Section 97 of P.D. 1445.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive strictly enforce and monitor compliance with the reporting requirement on the sources and utilization of LDRRMF pursuant to Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that the BAC procure POL of the Municipality strictly in accordance with RA No. 9184 and its revised IRR. Specifically, we recommend the following:

a. 	Procure POL through competitive bidding process. Resort to alternative methods of procurement only when justified by circumstances specifically provided in RA No. 9184, its revised IRR, and GPPB issuances.


b. 	Discontinue reimbursement as mode of payment for POL.


c. 	Include POL in the offices’ PPMPs and the consolidated APP.


d. 	Itemize and detail technical specifications of POL products to be procured in invitations to bid/requests for quotations. Other terms and requirements, which lie within the prerogative and sound discretion of the procuring entity, be included for POL procurement. Likewise reflect these specifications, terms and requirements in the purchase orders/contracts of agreement.

We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator set up and maintain a GAD database containing complete data requirements enumerated in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01. The GFPS TWG use sex-disaggregated data and other GAD-related information in developing a gender-responsive GAD Plan and Budget.

We recommended that the Municipality strictly implement the project on reduction of plastic bags usage under the solid waste management program. 


We also recommend that separate appropriations for the MENRO be provided and a full-time MENR Officer, either a Department Head or OIC meantime that finances do not permit, be appointed to carry out all other environmental programs.

We recommended that the Accounting Unit analyze the composition of Accounts Payable and other recognized obligations which remained unclaimed for more than two years. Revert the amounts to unappropriated surplus through proper legislative authority particularly those without outstanding claims or not supported with valid documents. Henceforth, we recommend that the setting up of payables be based on valid and authorized obligations duly supported with proper obligating documents.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the MPDC to conduct regular monitoring and evaluation of the 20% Development Fund projects in order to achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes for the benefit of the intended beneficiaries. Also, mobilize the concerned offices in the early planning and implementation of development projects to avoid incurring possible additional cost resulting in delayed implementation.

We recommend that management exert effort to enforce the immediate settlement of audit disallowances by sending demand letters to the persons liable. For those personnel who are still in service, the Local Chief Executive should demand the immediate settlement of disallowance by directing the Municipal Treasurer to withhold payment of the salary and other emoluments to the satisfaction of the amounts disallowed.

We recommend that all contracts of agreement be renewed annually and notarized to ensure the validity and compliance with the terms, particularly on payment of rent, sublease, maintenance and prohibition on making the stalls as dwelling place.  Automatic cancellation of contract shall be enforced when the lessee continuously failed to pay rentals on the third month and to apply the deposit and advance payment to rentals due for payment.  Extensions and expansions shall not be allowed unless proper dues are assessed and collected on time.

We recommend that the Accounting Office review records on real property taxes and adjust the receivable and deferred credit accounts to arrive at the correct balance.  The receivable account is supported with the Certified List of Taxpayers based on Real Property Tax Account Register prepared by the Municipal Treasurer’s Office. We also enjoin the preparation of Certified List of Delinquent Taxpayers to determine the accuracy of recorded RPT/SET Receivable balances as of end of the year and to facilitate collection of receivables.  We also recommend that the collection target on real property taxes set by BLGF be observed or the amount equivalent to 1% of the total value of real property appearing in the Real Property Tax Account Register /Taxpayer’s index card be recorded. Efforts must be exerted to work back on prior years’ record to review its accuracy and ensure correctness of journal entries.  Guidelines in recording income, adjustments and establishing liability accounts should be diligently observed, to avoid confusions, and produce accurate financial statements.

We recommend that projects funded from the above enumerated programs be specifically identified and corresponding program of work or activity proposal finalized for implementation.  We enjoined strict implementation of the guidelines on projects to be funded from 20% development funds as enumerated in Joint Memorandum Circular 2011-1.  Henceforth, development projects must be supported with program design or program of work or activity proposal before submission to the executive office for inclusion in the proposed budget to ensure adequacy of appropriation for its implementation and completion.

We recommend that management should strictly comply with the submission of contracts/purchase orders and all documents forming part thereof to the Auditor, within five (5) working days from execution to facilitate review processes, generate timely and relevant audit results and avoid enforcement of sanctions prescribed by law.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned offices to submit purchase requests on time so as the BAC Secretariat could consolidate all purchase requests and adopt competitive bidding in the procurement of supplies and materials to assure that lowest possible price is obtained for such procurement.  Alternative methods of procurement shall be resorted to only in highly exceptional cases provided under the guidelines of RA 9184.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer be required to prepare the Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (CRAAF) and to submit the same together with the individual RAAF of all collectors in accordance with Section 50 and 51 of the NGAS Manual, Volume II. 
We  also recommend that every time collectors will remit their daily collections, the Liquidating Officer/Municipal Treasurer shall require the presentation of the current and unused stubs to validate the last Official Receipt issued as appearing in the Report of Collection and Deposits and to sign   the last Official Receipt issued.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer should strictly require all collecting clerks to turn over their collections at the close of each business day. Ensure that all collections are completely remitted by checking the accountable forms daily. The Treasurer/Liquidating Officer should strictly comply with Section 32 of NGAS-Local to prevent any untoward incident to happen while so much cash is kept in the vault of the Treasurer’s Office. Compliance with the provisions of Sections 29 and 32 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for LGUs, Volume 1, should be strictly enforced, to protect government funds from risk of loss and/or misapplication.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant and other concerned LGU officials in charge of the disbursement and monitoring of the financial assistance should require the recipient agencies/ organizations to submit a Fund Utilization Report together with supporting documents. Moreover, the recipient NGO should comply with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2007-001 to ascertain the attainment of objectives for which the funds were released as well as the propriety of expenditures.

We recommended that the management cancel checks long outstanding in the General Fund (GF) and Special Education Fund (SEF), revert the amounts to the unappropriated surplus of the fund and adjust its books for fair presentation of Cash in Bank in the Financial Statements pursuant to the provision of Section 59 of the New Government Accounting Manual System (NGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs) Volume 1 and Section 97 of P.D. 1445.
