observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipality strictly implement the project on reduction of plastic bags usage under the solid waste management program. 


We also recommend that separate appropriations for the MENRO be provided and a full-time MENR Officer, either a Department Head or OIC meantime that finances do not permit, be appointed to carry out all other environmental programs.
observations.managementAction: The municipality’s project on reduction of plastic bags usage was not strictly implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive cause the immediate liquidation of the outstanding cash advances. Additional cash advances shall not be granted to officials and employees unless their previous cash advances are liquidated. The Municipal Accountant diligently monitor and enforce settlement thereof and if necessary, management deduct the amount of unliquidated cash advances from the claims of the concerned officials and employees.

We recommended that the Accounting Unit analyze the composition of Accounts Payable and other recognized obligations which remained unclaimed for more than two years. Revert the amounts to unappropriated surplus through proper legislative authority particularly those without outstanding claims or not supported with valid documents. Henceforth, we recommend that the setting up of payables be based on valid and authorized obligations duly supported with proper obligating documents.
observations.managementAction: There were still accounts payable without details existing in the books as of December 31, 2017.
observations.reason: Observation first raised in CY 2011 AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the MPDC to conduct regular monitoring and evaluation of the 20% Development Fund projects in order to achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes for the benefit of the intended beneficiaries. Also, mobilize the concerned offices in the early planning and implementation of development projects to avoid incurring possible additional cost resulting in delayed implementation.
observations.managementAction: Huge balances of appropriations for 20% DF still remain unutilized.
observations.reason: Observation first raised in CY 2012 AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management exert effort to enforce the immediate settlement of audit disallowances by sending demand letters to the persons liable. For those personnel who are still in service, the Local Chief Executive should demand the immediate settlement of disallowance by directing the Municipal Treasurer to withhold payment of the salary and other emoluments to the satisfaction of the amounts disallowed.
observations.managementAction: The persons liable were no longer connected with the LGU.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that all contracts of agreement be renewed annually and notarized to ensure the validity and compliance with the terms, particularly on payment of rent, sublease, maintenance and prohibition on making the stalls as dwelling place.  Automatic cancellation of contract shall be enforced when the lessee continuously failed to pay rentals on the third month and to apply the deposit and advance payment to rentals due for payment.  Extensions and expansions shall not be allowed unless proper dues are assessed and collected on time.

We also recommend that the Administrative Aide VI/Revenue Collecting Officer in charge of computing and assessing the stall holders’ power consumption work back from January 2013 to July 2013 to properly adjust the recorded electricity expense of the municipality.  The recording of receivable account reduces the expenses and adjusts the recorded miscellaneous income when payment of electric bills from stall holders is received. The power rate per kilowatt shall also be adjusted depending on the applicable power rate during the month.  

The Municipal Treasurer should also consider the idea of adjusting the contract period for stall rentals, commencing the month of January,  so the same coincides with the calendar year and fiscal year observed by the government.   There is also a need to regularly check power lines in the market stalls and its premises as a deterrent against pilferage and illegal connections.
observations.managementAction:
Renewal of stalls contract was reviewed annually and compliance with the provisions of the contract were strictly monitored. 





Fully Implemented
Electrical connection of the market stalls were separated from the Municipality.





Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Accounting Office review records on real property taxes and adjust the receivable and deferred credit accounts to arrive at the correct balance.  The receivable account is supported with the Certified List of Taxpayers based on Real Property Tax Account Register prepared by the Municipal Treasurer’s Office. We also enjoin the preparation of Certified List of Delinquent Taxpayers to determine the accuracy of recorded RPT/SET Receivable balances as of end of the year and to facilitate collection of receivables.  We also recommend that the collection target on real property taxes set by BLGF be observed or the amount equivalent to 1% of the total value of real property appearing in the Real Property Tax Account Register /Taxpayer’s index card be recorded. Efforts must be exerted to work back on prior years’ record to review its accuracy and ensure correctness of journal entries.  Guidelines in recording income, adjustments and establishing liability accounts should be diligently observed, to avoid confusions, and produce accurate financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Review of records on real property taxes and special education taxes were on-going.
observations.reason: Observation first raised in CY 2010 AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that projects funded from the above enumerated programs be specifically identified and corresponding program of work or activity proposal finalized for implementation.  We enjoined strict implementation of the guidelines on projects to be funded from 20% development funds as enumerated in Joint Memorandum Circular 2011-1.  Henceforth, development projects must be supported with program design or program of work or activity proposal before submission to the executive office for inclusion in the proposed budget to ensure adequacy of appropriation for its implementation and completion.
observations.managementAction: No Program of Works and Program Designs was prepared to support the programs/projects/ activities enumerated in the AIP.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management should strictly comply with the submission of contracts/purchase orders and all documents forming part thereof to the Auditor, within five (5) working days from execution to facilitate review processes, generate timely and relevant audit results and avoid enforcement of sanctions prescribed by law.
observations.managementAction: Some POs were not submitted to the Audit Team Leader for review.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer be required to prepare the Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (CRAAF) and to submit the same together with the individual RAAF of all collectors in accordance with Section 50 and 51 of the NGAS Manual, Volume II. 
We  also recommend that every time collectors will remit their daily collections, the Liquidating Officer/Municipal Treasurer shall require the presentation of the current and unused stubs to validate the last Official Receipt issued as appearing in the Report of Collection and Deposits and to sign   the last Official Receipt issued.
observations.managementAction: The CRAAF is not regularly made and submitted to the Auditor’s Office.  The Report is only submitted when requested, especially in preparation of the conduct of cash examination.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer should strictly require all collecting clerks to turn over their collections at the close of each business day. Ensure that all collections are completely remitted by checking the accountable forms daily. The Treasurer/Liquidating Officer should strictly comply with Section 32 of NGAS-Local to prevent any untoward incident to happen while so much cash is kept in the vault of the Treasurer’s Office. Compliance with the provisions of Sections 29 and 32 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for LGUs, Volume 1, should be strictly enforced, to protect government funds from risk of loss and/or misapplication.
observations.managementAction: Some collecting clerks still failed to remit their collections regularly and intact.  Large remittances were observed before the conduct of cash examination and low remittance made during enrollment days.  No sanction is imposed on the erring collecting officer.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant and other concerned LGU officials in charge of the disbursement and monitoring of the financial assistance should require the recipient agencies/ organizations to submit a Fund Utilization Report together with supporting documents. Moreover, the recipient NGO should comply with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2007-001 to ascertain the attainment of objectives for which the funds were released as well as the propriety of expenditures.
observations.managementAction: Concerned organizations/ agencies failed to submit liquidation reports to date due to lack of monitoring on the part of the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive assign the Municipal Assessor to process the titles of these properties in the name of the LGU and should be properly kept by the general services officer or the local treasurer.  Moreover, the Accounting Unit should reconcile records per books with the actual value of lots owned by the LGU.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor was already instructed to inventory lots owned by the municipality and initiate action to process its title.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this year’s AAR, Obs. No. 2

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Accounting Office review the composition of the Construction in Progress account and adjust those that are already complete.  Henceforth, observe the proper accounting of assets constructed by the municipality as  provided in the above regulation.
observations.managementAction: The projects could not be identified due to lack of documents
observations.reason: Finding was first raised in CY 2013 AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that an inspection be made on the condition of the biometric machines and immediately install and require its use.  Enjoin all employees to register their finger readings with the machine   and start its use.   The procedure records the actual arrival and departure of employees in the office, thus avoiding overpayment in salaries and wages.  For future transactions, avoid purchasing supplies and equipments when its use is not immediate, to avoid wastage of government resources.
observations.managementAction: The Officials and Employees still used the Attendance Logbook inspite of having the two (2) Biometric Machines
observations.reason: Finding was first raised in CY 2013 AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned offices to submit purchase requests on time so as the BAC Secretariat could consolidate all purchase requests and adopt competitive bidding in the procurement of supplies and materials to assure that lowest possible price is obtained for such procurement.  Alternative methods of procurement shall be resorted to only in highly exceptional cases provided under the guidelines of RA9184.
observations.managementAction: PR’s are submitted to the Supply Office when there is need for such supplies.
observations.reason: Finding was first raised in CY 2012 AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management should exert effort in securing the records of the Accounts Receivable which remained outstanding for more than ten years and enforce its collection.  In the event that its collections are improbable, procedures prescribed pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-001 dated February 5, 1997 should be followed.
observations.managementAction: No action has been taken to collect the outstanding receivables
observations.reason: Finding was first raised in CY 2012 AAR.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive direct the conduct of physical count of the LGU’s PPE by an Inventory Committee and require submission of an updated and complete Report of Physical Count. Likewise, the Accountant and Property Officer maintain and reconcile the required PPE records to establish the existence and accuracy of balances of PPE in the financial statements. Also, furnish a copy of such report to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended that the management cancel checks long outstanding in the General Fund (GF) and Special Education Fund (SEF), revert the amounts to the unappropriated surplus of the fund and adjust its books for fair presentation of Cash in Bank in the Financial Statements pursuant to the provision of Section 59 of the New Government Accounting Manual System () for Local Government Units (LGUs) Volume 1 and Section 97 of P.D. 1445.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive strictly enforce and monitor compliance with the reporting requirement on the sources and utilization of LDRRMF pursuant to Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant transfer the unexpended/unobligated balance of Quick Response Fund and DRRM – MOOE for calendar year 2016 to the Special Trust Fund in the Trust Fund books in accordance with Accounting and reporting guidelines of the LDRRMF provided in COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the Municipal School Board formulate the school board budget strictly in accordance with the provisions of RA 7160 and DepEd – DBM – DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 and its implementing regulations in the utilization of the SEF. Only items among the required and priority projects enumerated in the regulations be allowed to be funded out of SEF. Responsible officials cease and desist charging expenditures of the office of the district supervisor from the school board budget.

We recommended that responsible officials, as indicated in Table II above, immediately submit accounts and reports to the Office of the Auditor to facilitate timely conduct of audit. Provisions of PD No. 1445, pertinent COA circulars and the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs on the submission of accounts and reports be strictly observed.

We recommended that the BAC procure POL of the Municipality strictly in accordance with RA No. 9184 and its revised IRR. Specifically, we recommend the following:
Procure POL through competitive bidding process. Resort to alternative methods of procurement only when justified by circumstances specifically provided in RA No. 9184, its revised IRR, and GPPB issuances.
Discontinue reimbursement as mode of payment for POL.
Include POL in the offices’ PPMPs and the consolidated APP.
Itemize and detail technical specifications of POL products to be procured in invitations to bid/requests for quotations. Other terms and requirements, which lie within the prerogative and sound discretion of the procuring entity, be included for POL procurement. Likewise reflect these specifications, terms and requirements in the purchase orders/contracts of agreement.

We recommended the following:
Organize the Municipal Tourism Council and Tourism Planning Committee to prepare the Local Tourism Development Plan that integrates zoning, land use, infrastructure development, the national system of standards for tourism enterprises, heritage and environmental protection imperatives in a manner that encourages sustainable tourism development.
Appoint a Local Tourism Officer that has the technical expertise in tourism planning and development. Tap other personnel of the Municipality that have the same expertise.
Prepare the profiling of its local tourism industry which includes the list of tourist attractions and activities, transportation, accommodation, other facilities and services, institutional elements and other tourist infrastructure and profiling of tourist-visitor, as well as the profiling of privately-owned beaches and resorts.
Increase the appropriation for Local Tourism Planning and Development to develop and manage tourism in a controlled and sustainable way.

We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator set up and maintain a GAD database containing complete data requirements enumerated in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01. The GFPS TWG use sex-disaggregated data and other GAD-related information in developing a gender-responsive GAD Plan and Budget.

We recommended that the Municipality strictly implement the project on reduction of plastic bags usage under the solid waste management program. We also recommend that separate appropriations for the MENRO be provided and a full-time MENR Officer, either a Department Head or OIC meantime that finances do not permit, be appointed to carry out all other environmental programs.

We recommended that management immediately submit documents showing that the payments of additional honoraria by the Municipality to the MBOC, RCG and Support Staff were authorized and with legal basis. Otherwise, payments will be disallowed in audit and persons liable will be made to refund the amounts received.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive cause/direct the conduct of physical count of the LGU’s PPE by an Inventory Committee, require submission of updated and complete Report of Physical Count maintenance of required PPE records by the accountant and property officer and reconciliation with accounting records to establish the existence and accuracy of balances of PPE in the financial statements. Also, furnish a copy of such report to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.managementAction: As of date, the Inventory Committee had conducted partial physical inventory of its properties.
observations.reason:
Finding was first raised in CY 2012 AAR.
Reiterated in this current years’ Annual Audit Report
